places.name: Royal Palace
places.desc: Iconic royal residence with Khmer architecture.

places.name: National Museum
places.desc: Home to Cambodia's finest artifacts.

places.name: Independence Monument
places.desc: A majestic lotus-shaped tower commemorating Cambodia's liberation from French rule.

places.name: Wat Phnom
places.desc: Historic hilltop temple and city landmark.

places.name: Toul Sleng Genocide Museum
places.desc: A former high school turned prison that serves as a haunting memorial to the Khmer Rouge era.

places.name: Choeung Ek Genocidal Center
places.desc: A peaceful yet somber memorial site located at the former 'Killing Fields' outside the city.

places.name: Sosoro Museum
places.desc: An interactive museum showcasing 2,000 years of Cambodia's monetary and economic history.

places.name: Bassac Lane
places.desc: A trendy hidden alleyway famous for its vibrant cocktail bars and boutique eateries.

places.name: Sunset Cruise
places.desc: A relaxing boat journey along the Tonle Sap and Mekong rivers to enjoy the city's evening skyline.

places.name: Chaktomuk Walk Street
places.desc: A popular pedestrian zone located along the Sisowath Quay Riverside which is a vibrant hub of street foods, handicrafts, performances, and crowds.

places.name: Central Market
places.desc: The Central Market of Phnom Penh, is a large market constructed in 1937 in the shape of a dome with four arms branching out into vast hallways with countless stalls of goods.

places.name: Russian Market
places.desc: A bustling local bazaar famous for souvenir hunting, clothing, and authentic street food.

places.name: Aeon Mall Phnom Penh
places.desc: A modern shopping complex offering international brands, diverse dining, and entertainment.

places.name: Garden City Water Park
places.desc: A large, family-friendly destination featuring thrilling slides and wave pools.

places.name: Phnom Penh Safari
places.desc: A wildlife park offering animal encounters and live shows in a natural setting.

places.name: Kol Tor Teung Pagoda
places.desc: A beautiful and serene Buddhist temple known for its intricate carvings and peaceful atmosphere.

places.name: Angkor Wat Temple
places.desc: The world's largest religious monument and a masterpiece of Khmer architecture, famous for its breathtaking sunrise views and intricate bas-reliefs.

places.name: Bayon Temple
places.desc: Located at the heart of Angkor Thom, this mesmerizing temple is renowned for its 54 gothic towers decorated with over 200 giant smiling stone faces.

places.name: Ta Prohm Temple
places.desc: Left to the elements, this iconic 'Tomb Raider' temple is famously swallowed by massive jungle silk-cotton trees and strangler figs.

places.name: Preah Khan Temple
places.desc: A sprawling monastic complex featuring corridors overgrown with trees and intricate carvings.

places.name: Neak Poan Temple
places.desc: A unique island temple set in a man-made pond, once used for medicinal rituals.

places.name: Pre Rup Temple
places.desc: A grand pyramid-shaped brick temple that offers spectacular views of the jungle at sunset.

places.name: Beng Mealea Temple
places.desc: An atmospheric, unrestored jungle temple that gives visitors a 'lost city' adventure feel.

places.name: Banteay Srei Temple
places.desc: A 10th-century Hindu temple celebrated for its miniature scale and pink sandstone carvings.

places.name: Banteay Samre
places.desc: A well-preserved temple featuring the same style as Angkor Wat without the heavy crowds.

places.name: Phimeanakas Temple
places.desc: A Hindu temple in the shape of a tiered pyramid located within ancient Royal Palace walls.

places.name: Terrace of the Elephants
places.desc: An impressive 300-meter platform adorned with carved elephants used for ceremonies.

places.name: Kulen Mountain
places.desc: A sacred site featuring a massive reclining Buddha, riverbed carvings, and waterfalls.

places.name: Bakheng Mountain Hill
places.desc: The premier spot for sunset views overlooking the distant towers of Angkor Wat.

places.name: Pub Street
places.desc: Nightlife hub with food and bars.

places.name: Kampong Phluk Floating Village
places.desc: A unique community of three stilted villages built on the Tonle Sap lake, about 16 km southeast of Siem Reap, Cambodia.

places.name: Preah Dak Village
places.desc: Renowned as a 'One Village One Product' destination which famous for palm cakes (Num Tnaot), fresh Num Banh Chok (Khmer noodles), and local crafts.

places.name: APOPO Visit Center
places.desc: An inspiring center where 'HeroRATs' are trained to detect landmines and save lives.

places.name: Angkor National Museum
places.desc: A world-class museum using interactive technology to explain the golden era of the Khmer Empire.

places.name: Artisans Silk Farm
places.desc: An educational site where visitors watch the entire process of traditional silk production.

places.name: Angkor's Zipline
places.desc: An eco-adventure through the rainforest canopy located within the historic Angkor Park.

places.name: Local Cuisine Classes
places.desc: A hands-on experience learning the secrets of traditional Khmer dishes like Amok and Lok Lak.

places.name: Phare, The Cambodian Circus
places.desc: A high-energy show combining theater and music to tell modern Cambodian stories.

places.name: Angkor Wildlife & Aquarium
places.desc: A massive conservation park featuring rare freshwater fish and majestic land animals.

places.name: Apsara Theatre
places.desc: An elegant venue to enjoy traditional Khmer dance performances paired with a classic dinner.

places.name: Preah Vihear Temple
places.desc: A stunning clifftop Hindu temple offering breathtaking views from the border.

places.name: Koh Ker Temple
places.desc: A remote archaeological site dominated by a massive seven-tiered pyramid hidden in the forest.

places.name: Preah Khan Kompong Svay
places.desc: One of Cambodia's largest and most remote temple complexes, perfect for exploration.

places.name: BeTreed Adventures
places.desc: An eco-tourism destination offering treehouse stays and ziplining in a protected forest.

places.name: Preah Nimith Waterfall
places.desc: A powerful and wide waterfall on the Mekong River near the Laos border.

places.name: Kulen Prumep Wildlife Sanctuary
places.desc: A vast protected area home to rare birds and traditional community-based ecotourism.

places.name: Bamboo Train
places.desc: Experience a fun and unique ride on a 'norry'—a handmade bamboo platform that skims along traditional railway tracks.

places.name: Phnom Sampeau
places.desc: A stunning mountain site featuring hilltop pagodas, historic caves, and panoramic views of the surrounding countryside.

places.name: Bat Cave
places.desc: Witness a spectacular natural phenomenon as millions of bats emerge from the caves in a massive, flowing stream at sunset.

places.name: Colonial Buildings
places.desc: Stroll through the downtown area to admire one of the best-preserved collections of French-era colonial architecture in Cambodia.

places.name: Romcheik 5 Art Space & Cafe
places.desc: A vibrant creative hub that serves as the heart of Battambang's contemporary local art scene.

places.name: Human Gallery
places.desc: A poignant photography gallery showcasing expressive portraits that highlight the humanity and stories of local residents.

places.name: Wat Banan Temple
places.desc: An 11th-century hilltop temple complex that bears a striking architectural resemblance to the famous Angkor Wat.

places.name: Wat Ek Phnom
places.desc: A beautiful 11th-century temple ruin featuring intricate sandstone carvings and a massive seated Buddha statue.

places.name: Ta Dumbong Kro Aung Statue
places.desc: An iconic landmark statue depicting the legendary 'stick king' who is central to the history and folklore of the city.

places.name: Koh Rong
places.desc: A stunning island paradise famous for its crystal-clear turquoise waters, vibrant coral reefs, and pristine white sandy beaches.

places.name: Koh Rong Samloem
places.desc: A tranquil and secluded island escape, perfect for travelers seeking peaceful bays and a relaxed, natural atmosphere.

places.name: Otres Beach
places.desc: A laid-back and quiet coastal stretch, known for its friendly beach bars and beautiful, gentle sunsets.

places.name: Serendipity Beach
places.desc: The city's most popular and lively beachfront, buzzing with restaurants, nightlife, and energetic crowds.

places.name: Sunset Beach
places.desc: A hidden gem tucked away on Koh Rong Samloem, offering the perfect secluded spot to watch the sun dip below the horizon.

places.name: Independence Beach
places.desc: A clean and well-maintained beach that offers a quieter, more refined seaside experience compared to the central hubs.

places.name: Ream National Park
places.desc: A vast protected sanctuary consisting of lush mangroves, tropical forests, and diverse coastal wildlife.

places.name: Kbal Chhay Waterfall
places.desc: A refreshing series of cascading waterfalls surrounded by lush greenery, perfect for a picnic or a cool afternoon swim.

places.name: Phsar Leu Market
places.desc: The largest traditional market in the city, offering a bustling atmosphere filled with fresh produce, local snacks, and goods.

places.name: 7Deuce 72 Poker
places.desc: A popular social spot for gaming and entertainment, providing a lively environment for visitors and locals alike.

places.name: Bokor National Park
places.desc: A sprawling protected area featuring a cool mountain climate, historic colonial ruins, and breathtaking cliffside views.

places.name: Anlung Pring Community
places.desc: An eco-tourism site dedicated to the conservation of Sarus Cranes and traditional local livelihoods.

places.name: La Plantation
places.desc: An award-winning organic farm where you can learn about the history and cultivation of world-famous Kampot pepper.

places.name: Kampot Riverside
places.desc: The scenic heart of the town, perfect for sunset strolls, firefly cruises, and relaxing waterfront dining.

places.name: Salt Fields
places.desc: Expansive coastal flats where sea water is evaporated to produce high-quality natural sea salt using traditional methods.

places.name: Durian Farm Kampot
places.desc: Lush plantations showcasing the region's most famous fruit, known for its distinctively rich and creamy flavor.

places.name: Tada Waterfall
places.desc: A refreshing natural escape featuring cascading waters and cool swimming pools surrounded by dense forest.

places.name: Teuk Chou Rapids
places.desc: A popular riverside retreat where locals and travelers gather to picnic and swim in the flowing currents.

places.name: Popokvil Waterfall
places.desc: A majestic two-tier waterfall located on Bokor Mountain, often shrouded in a beautiful misty cloud.

places.name: Arcadia Waterpark
places.desc: An adventurous riverside park featuring giant slides, blobbing, and various water sports for thrill-seekers.

places.name: Potshots
places.desc: A vibrant riverside bar and social spot known for its fun atmosphere, games, and great views of the Kampot river.

places.name: Kep Beach
places.desc: A peaceful, quiet coastal beach lined with swaying trees, perfect for a relaxing seaside stroll.

places.name: Koh Tonsay
places.desc: Also known as Rabbit Island, this destination offers rustic island life, scenic hiking trails, and beautiful ocean views.

places.name: Kep Butterfly Farm
places.desc: A charming nature attraction where visitors can walk through a lush garden filled with diverse local butterfly species.

places.name: Flying Dog Farm
places.desc: A serene, family-run farm that provides a relaxing nature experience away from the busier tourist spots.

places.name: River Cruise Tour
places.desc: A scenic boat tour offering a relaxing way to enjoy the coastal breezes and see the town from the water.

places.name: Kampong Trach Mountain Cave
places.desc: A mystical cave complex nestled within limestone mountains, featuring hidden shrines and natural rock formations.

places.name: Crab Market
places.desc: A vibrant riverside market famous for fresh blue crab and authentic local seafood dining experiences.

places.name: Dolphin Kayaking Adventures
places.desc: An eco-friendly way to navigate the Mekong and catch a rare glimpse of the endangered Irrawaddy dolphins in their natural habitat.

places.name: Mekong River
places.desc: The lifeblood of the region, offering stunning riverside views and spectacular sunsets over the wide, flowing waters.

places.name: Koh Trong
places.desc: A peaceful island in the middle of the Mekong where visitors can explore pomelo orchards and experience a traditional rural lifestyle by bicycle.

places.name: 100 Columns Pagodas
places.desc: A significant historical and religious site known for its beautiful architecture and its connection to local legends.

places.name: Cardamom Mountains
places.desc: One of Southeast Asia's largest remaining rainforests, offering a rugged paradise for trekking and rare wildlife spotting.

places.name: Tatai Waterfall
places.desc: A stunning semi-circular waterfall located along the Tatai River, perfect for a refreshing swim in the heart of the jungle.

places.name: Mangrove Forest
places.desc: An expansive coastal sanctuary where you can walk through raised boardwalks or kayak through winding emerald waterways.

places.name: Koh Kong Island
places.desc: An untouched gem featuring long stretches of white sand and crystal-clear water, ideal for those seeking a true island escape.

places.name: River Safari
places.desc: A peaceful boat journey that takes you deep into the wilderness to witness the region's diverse ecosystem and natural beauty.

places.name: Bou Sra Waterfall
places.desc: Cambodia's most famous triple-tiered waterfall, offering a majestic display of nature within a lush, tropical forest setting.

places.name: Elephant Sanctuary
places.desc: A dedicated conservation area where visitors can observe rescued elephants roaming freely and learning about ethical wildlife protection.

places.name: Sen Monorom
places.desc: A charming highland town known for its cool climate, pine trees, and as the gateway to Cambodia's eastern wilderness.

places.name: Hill Views
places.desc: Breathtaking panoramic vistas of rolling green hills often referred to as the 'Switzerland of Cambodia' for their unique landscape.

places.name: Jungle Trekking
places.desc: An immersive journey through dense forests and ethnic Bunong villages to discover hidden waterfalls and diverse local flora.

places.name: Yeak Laom Lake
places.desc: A stunning volcanic crater lake known for its crystal-clear emerald water and serene jungle surroundings.

places.name: Virachey National Park
places.desc: One of Cambodia's largest protected areas, offering deep-jungle trekking and rare wildlife encounters.

places.name: Cha Ong Waterfall
places.desc: A majestic waterfall set in a rocky canyon where visitors can walk behind the curtain of falling water.

places.name: Ka Tieng Waterfall
places.desc: A picturesque cascade surrounded by lush forest, popular for swimming and relaxing in nature.

places.name: Banlung Town
places.desc: The red-dirt capital of the province and a central hub for exploring the surrounding natural wonders.

places.name: Banteay Chhmar Temple
places.desc: A massive, remote temple complex famous for its intricate carvings and unique multi-faced towers.

places.name: Sisophon Town
places.desc: The bustling provincial capital that serves as a key crossroads between Thailand and Siem Reap.

places.name: Ang Trapeang Thmor
places.desc: A vast reservoir and bird sanctuary that is a critical habitat for the rare Sarus Crane.

places.name: Svay Mountain
places.desc: A local hilltop site featuring pagodas and offering panoramic views of the surrounding plains.

places.name: Bamboo Bridge
places.desc: An incredible seasonal structure rebuilt every year to connect the city to Koh Paen island.

places.name: Nokor Bachey Temple
places.desc: A unique 11th-century sandstone temple with a modern colorful pagoda built directly inside its ruins.

places.name: French Lighthouse
places.desc: A historic colonial-era landmark offering a bird's-eye view of the Mekong River and its surroundings.

places.name: Mekong Riverside
places.desc: A wide, scenic promenade perfect for evening walks, local snacks, and enjoying the river breeze.

places.name: Koh Paen Island
places.desc: A peaceful river island ideal for cycling through traditional villages and pomelo orchards.

places.name: Floating Village
places.desc: A fascinating community of colorful houses and markets that drift along the Tonle Sap river.

places.name: Pottery Village
places.desc: The heart of Cambodia's clay craft, where artisans still use traditional methods to make various clay pots.

places.name: Phnom Da Temple
places.desc: An ancient hilltop site with ruins that date back to the Funan era, overlooking scenic floodplains.

places.name: Neang Kang Rey Mountain
places.desc: Known for its silhouette resembling a reclining woman and is associated with a tragic Khmer legend from the Twelve Sisters story.

places.name: Kirirom National Park
places.desc: A high-altitude pine forest reserve known for its cool air, waterfalls, and scenic walking trails.

places.name: Chambok Waterfall
places.desc: An eco-tourism site featuring a high cascade reachable by a pleasant trek through the forest.

places.name: Teuk Pus Hot Spring
places.desc: A rare natural hot spring believed to have healing properties, located in a quiet rural setting.

places.name: Phnom Aural
places.desc: The highest peak in Cambodia, offering a challenging and rewarding trek for experienced hikers.

places.name: Knong Psar (Khnang Phsar)
places.desc: Famed for lush green grasslands, pine forests, and dramatic sunrise views, it offers 2–3 day hiking and camping adventures popular during the rainy season.

places.name: Sambor Prei Kuk Temple
places.desc: A UNESCO World Heritage site featuring pre-Angkorian brick temples scattered through a quiet forest.

places.name: Stung Sen River
places.desc: A major waterway that offers scenic boat trips and insights into the local riverside communities.

places.name: Prasat Andet
places.desc: An 8th-century brick temple known for its distinct architecture and historical significance.

places.name: Santuk Mountain
places.desc: Located in Ko Koh village, Ko Koh commune, Santuk District, it is the most sacred mountain of the province.

places.name: Oudong Mountain
places.desc: The former royal capital of Cambodia, featuring impressive stupas and panoramic views of the countryside.

places.name: Ta Khmao Town
places.desc: The provincial capital, offering a blend of modern development and traditional riverside charm.

places.name: Koh Dach
places.desc: A tranquil 'Silk Island' on the Mekong River known for traditional weaving and rural charm.

places.name: Dangrek Mountains
places.desc: A rugged mountain range forming a natural border, offering historical sites and scenic overlooks.

places.name: Anlong Veng
places.desc: A town with significant modern history, serving as a gateway to exploring the last strongholds of the Khmer Rouge.

places.name: Pol Pot Tomb
places.desc: A simple historical site that marks a significant and somber chapter in Cambodia's past.

places.name: Wat Phnom Yat
places.desc: A revered hilltop temple known for its Burmese-style architecture and stunning views of the town.

places.name: Pailin Gem Market
places.desc: A unique market where you can find the precious rubies and sapphires for which Pailin is historically famous.

places.name: Elephant Head Waterfall
places.desc: Often called Kbal Domri Waterfall, is located in Sangkhat Bar Yakha, Pailin municipality far about ~8km away from Grandma Yat Statue.

places.name: Phnom Khiev Waterfall
places.desc: A scenic mountain area offering cool breezes and a peaceful escape into nature.

places.name: Ba Phnom
places.desc: A sacred hill with ancient ruins and pagodas, considered the birthplace of the Funan Kingdom.

places.name: Kompong Sne Dam
places.desc: A significant ecological site and reservoir known for its scenic water views and as a habitat for local bird species.

places.name: Weaving Handicraft Center
places.desc: A cultural hub where visitors can witness the intricate process of traditional Khmer silk and cotton weaving.

places.name: Prey Chong Srok Temple
places.desc: A peaceful ancient temple site that serves as an important spiritual landmark for the local provincial community.

places.name: Kampong Luong
places.desc: A vast and vibrant floating town on the Tonle Sap Lake, complete with its own schools, shops, and clinics.

places.name: Veal Veng
places.desc: A gateway to the Cardamom Mountains, offering rugged adventures through some of Southeast Asia's most biodiversity-rich wilderness.

places.name: L'bak Komronh
places.desc: A natural rocky rapid along the river that serves as a popular recreational spot for swimming and family picnics.

places.name: Osoam Cardamom Community Centre
places.desc: An eco-tourism center focused on conservation and community-led tours through the surrounding lush mountain landscapes.

places.name: Ramsar Wetlands
places.desc: An ecologically vital area of protected wetlands, home to many species of birds and aquatic life.

places.name: Sopheakmit Waterfall
places.desc: A powerful and wide waterfall located on the Mekong, famous for its dramatic spray and rock formations.

places.name: Preah Ko Temple
places.desc: An ancient brick temple ruin from the pre-Angkorian era, showcasing early Khmer architectural craftsmanship.

places.name: Phnom Preah Theat
places.desc: A hilltop sacred site offering panoramic views of the Mekong River and the dense forests of the northern province.

places.name: Waiko River
places.desc: A tranquil waterway that offers scenic views and insights into the agricultural life along its banks.

places.name: Bavet City
places.desc: A bustling border city known as a major international gateway, featuring vibrant markets and a unique cross-cultural atmosphere.

places.name: Phnom Chisor
places.desc: An 11th-century mountain temple offering expansive views and historical intrigue from the Angkorian era.

places.name: Chup Pol Temple
places.desc: A serene temple site built near a natural spring, known for its peaceful atmosphere and historical stone structures.

places.name: Phnom Da
places.desc: The site of a 6th-century temple that marks the transition into the Angkorian period, overlooking vast scenic floodplains.

places.name: Neang Khmao Temple
places.desc: Known as the 'Temple of the Black Virgin,' these sandstone ruins are dedicated to Hindu deities and date back to the 10th century.

places.name: Phnom Tamao Zoological Park
places.desc: Cambodia's premier wildlife rescue center, providing a vast natural habitat for a wide variety of rescued animals.

places.name: Wat Phnom Khliang
places.desc: A unique pagoda built directly into a cave system, combining natural rock formations with spiritual Khmer architecture.

places.name: Tonle Bati
places.desc: A popular lakeside retreat featuring well-preserved ancient temples and bamboo picnic huts along the water's edge.

places.name: Rubber Plantation
places.desc: Vast, orderly forest estates that have been a cornerstone of the provincial economy for generations.

places.name: Haong Waterfall
places.desc: A hidden natural gem that provides a quiet, cool escape for locals and adventurous travelers.

places.name: Preah Nor Kor Knong-Kau Temple
places.desc: The provincial capital, serving as a lively hub for commerce, administration, and local culture.
